fixture_notice: SYNTHETIC-DRY-RUN NON-EVIDENTIARY: this definition exists only to exercise the LCMRP foundational taxonomy contract.
definition: A constructed comparison subject containing two deliberately competing candidate organizations: Organization K assigns one series-stable primary functional kind, while Organization R assigns contextual, potentially many-valued functional roles.
boundary: The subject is limited to deterministic serialization of constructed cases. It does not establish category truth, completeness, utility, biological validity, external validity, mechanism effectiveness, or adoption readiness.

organizations.name: Stable kind-first partition
organizations.rule: Assign exactly one primary functional kind to each admitted series; cross-kind use requires a new or derived series, and WORKING is an availability tier.

organizations.name: Contextual role-first relation
organizations.rule: Assign zero, one, or multiple roles to an object version relative to a task, actor, purpose, and time; role change alone does not require a new version.
